title: Light Peruvian Pork Tenderloin Chupe
description: A vibrant, earthy, and energizing Peruvian dish featuring pork tenderloin, sweet potatoes, and purple corn. Perfect for a quick and healthy dinner or lunch.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together olive oil, cumin, salt, and sugar. Add the pork tenderloin and toss to coat.
In a separate bowl, boil the sweet potatoes in salted water until tender. Drain and set aside.
In a large skillet, heat a couple of tablespoons of olive oil over medium-high heat. Add the onion and red pepper and cook until tender, about 5 minutes.
Add the garlic and cook for an additional minute, stirring constantly.
Add the cooked sweet potatoes, purple corn, and cilantro to the skillet. Toss to combine.
Transfer the pork tenderloin to the oven and roast for 15-20 minutes, or until cooked to your desired level of doneness.
Remove the pork from the oven and let it rest for a few minutes before slicing.
To serve, place a serving of the sweet potato and corn mixture on a plate, then top with sliced pork tenderloin and drizzle with a bit of dark chocolate.
